shortDescription.text: Hardcoded session cookie secret in configuration

fullDescription.text: The session cookie secret is hardcoded as 'session_cookie_secret_key_here' in config/env/all.js. An attacker who reads the source code can forge session cookies, hijack any user session, and escalate privileges. Secrets must be loaded from environment variables or a secrets manager.

The cryptographic key is hardcoded as 'a_secure_key_for_crypto_here' in config/env/all.js. This key is used for encrypting sensitive data. Any attacker with source access can decrypt all protected data. Cryptographic keys must be externalized to environment variables or a KMS.

shortDescription.text: Entire configuration including secrets logged to console

fullDescription.text: config/config.js calls console.log(util.inspect(config)) which dumps the full configuration object—including cookieSecret, cryptoKey, and database credentials—to stdout. In containerized or cloud environments this output is captured by log aggregation services, exposing secrets to anyone with log access.

shortDescription.text: MongoDB connection string lacks authentication credentials

fullDescription.text: The default MongoDB connection string 'mongodb://localhost:27017/nodegoat' in config/env/all.js has no authentication. If MongoDB is exposed on a network interface (misconfigured bindIp or in a container network), any client can read, modify, or delete all data without credentials.

shortDescription.text: Potential path traversal via NODE_ENV in dynamic require

fullDescription.text: config/config.js constructs a file path using NODE_ENV: require(path.resolve(__dirname + '/../config/env/' + finalEnv.toLowerCase() + '.js')). If an attacker controls NODE_ENV (e.g., via process startup arguments or environment injection), they could set it to a value like '../../etc/passwd' to load arbitrary files, though the .js suffix limits exploitation. The toLowerCase() call does not sanitize path traversal characters.

shortDescription.text: Production config is empty, inheriting insecure development defaults

fullDescription.text: config/env/production.js exports an empty object, meaning production deployments inherit all insecure defaults from all.js—including the hardcoded cookieSecret, cryptoKey, and unauthenticated MongoDB URI. This defeats the purpose of environment-specific configuration and means production runs with known-weak secrets.

shortDescription.text: ZAP API key hardcoded in development configuration

fullDescription.text: config/env/development.js contains a hardcoded zapApiKey. While this is a development-only value, it establishes a pattern of storing API keys in source code. If this pattern is followed for production keys, they would be exposed in version control history.

shortDescription.text: Livereload script injected via document.write in development config

fullDescription.text: config/env/development.js uses environmentalScripts containing document.write to inject a livereload script. document.write is a DOM manipulation sink that can enable XSS if the injected content is ever influenced by user input. Additionally, loading scripts from localhost:35729 in a misconfigured environment could be hijacked.

message.text: Hardcoded session secret 'session_cookie_secret_key_here' allows session forgery and hijacking. Replace with process.env.SESSION_SECRET.
Hardcoded cryptographic key 'a_secure_key_for_crypto_here' compromises all encrypted data. Replace with process.env.CRYPTO_KEY or a KMS-managed key.
console.log(util.inspect(config)) dumps secrets (cookieSecret, cryptoKey, db credentials) to stdout. Remove this logging or redact sensitive fields.
MongoDB connection 'mongodb://localhost:27017/nodegoat' has no authentication. Enable MongoDB auth and use credentials from environment variables.
Dynamic require using NODE_ENV without path traversal sanitization. Validate NODE_ENV against an allowlist of known environments before constructing the path.
production.js is empty, so production inherits hardcoded secrets from all.js. Production must override cookieSecret, cryptoKey, and db with secure, externalized values.
ZAP API key hardcoded in development.js. Use environment variables for all API keys to prevent accidental exposure in version control.
document.write used to inject livereload script in development config. Use DOM API (createElement/appendChild) instead to avoid XSS sink.
